headline: Education Technology Spending Reaches RM2.1 Billion With Mixed Results
context: Government and school-level spending on education technology hit RM2.1 billion in 2025 but learning outcomes show no improvement.

cards.big: RM2.1 billion spent on education technology in 2025 but PISA scores did not improve
cards.sub: Device procurement dominated spending while teacher training received only 8%

cards.big: DELIMa platform has 4.2 million student accounts but average login rate is 23% weekly
cards.sub: Platform adoption remains low despite mandatory registration at all schools

cards.big: OECD research shows technology improves learning only when paired with pedagogy change
cards.sub: Hardware without instructional redesign produces expensive distraction not enhancement

cards.big: Uruguay's Plan Ceibal achieved measurable gains by investing 40% of tech budget on training
cards.sub: Teacher capability is the determining factor in educational technology effectiveness

cards.big: Buying devices without changing teaching is like buying instruments without teaching music
Should at least 30% of education technology budgets be mandated for teacher training
